extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH won a field sprint at Fougères, launched by MORKOV after BALLERINI crashed, beating PHILIPSEN and LAPORTE who changed shoes after a mechanical.
tactical_patterns: LAPORTE changed shoes mid-stage after mechanical, rejoined peloton, still finished 7th in sprint—indicates exceptional fitness reserve for flat stages.
VAN MOER bridged solo from break to contest sprint for crashed teammate EWAN, suggesting Lotto domestiques carry sprint speed reserve late-race.
BALLERINI lost leadout wheel but CAVENDISH still won, revealing Deceuninck's depth allows flexibility when primary train structure collapses.
Intermediate sprint 20 miles from finish created intense green jersey contest, fragmenting sprint trains before final—earlier intermediates drain sprint leadout cohesion.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: won after 3-year Tour absence and near-retirement, indicating peak form can return rapidly with proper team structure
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: crashed previous day with broken collarbone, absent from sprint—physical fragility pattern emerging
rider_signals.VAN MOER Brent: positive: bridged from breakaway to contest sprint solo for injured leader, showing rare dual breakaway-sprint capability
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: 7th place after mid-race shoe change mechanical demonstrates exceptional recovery ability and positioning resilience
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: negative: lost leadout wheel in final, structural fragility when Deceuninck train disrupted despite team depth
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: runner-up finish confirms consistent sprint placement but lacks winning acceleration against top-tier opposition
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: delivered winning leadout despite train disruption, confirms elite pilot reliability under chaotic conditions
